comparisons.visual: A hereditary estate set well back from the road: hedges, locking gates, broken greenhouses, a large shady garden of box-bordered paths and grape-covered arbors.…
A big airy room at the top of the house, nearly the whole floor. Windows look all ways-barred. Rings and things in the walls. A great heavy bedstead. The wallpa…
He smiles, already certain.
Later. THE NARRATOR sits by a window with her journal, writing in quick secret strokes. Sunshine pours in. Below: garden, arbors, a shaded lane toward a private…
He checks the barred windows, satisfied, and goes. She exhales, exhausted by the performance of calm.
Moonlight. THE NARRATOR lies on the nailed-down bed. John sleeps beside her. She stares at the wallpaper: lame uncertain curves that plunge off at outrageous an…
She rises softly, touches the wall. A draught. John stirs.
Box-bordered paths, long grape arbors with seats, riotous old-fashioned flowers, gnarly trees. THE NARRATOR walks slowly, supervised by distance. JENNIE-John's …
Two weeks in. THE NARRATOR at the window again, journal open. She is thinner in the face; the effort of dressing shows.
She looks toward a closed door-somewhere below, a baby cries once, then is soothed.
He takes her in his arms, fond, dismissing.
THE NARRATOR alone, tracing the pattern with her eyes from the bed. A recurrent spot: the pattern lolls like a broken neck; two bulbous eyes stare upside-down. …
JENNIE's figure appears far down the winding road. THE NARRATOR hides the journal at once. JENNIE enters with clean linen, cheerful, thorough.
JENNIE smiles, unconvinced, and begins to tidy. She smooths a yellow smooch on the baseboard without comment-yet.
JOHN sits beside her, professional kindness.
He doses her with cod-liver oil, tonics; pours a little wine. Rare meat waits on a tray.
She tries to speak earnestly. Tears come before she finishes.
Moonlight creeps in one window, then another. JOHN asleep. THE NARRATOR watches the undulating paper. Behind the outside pattern, a faint figure seems to shake …
He sits up straight-stern, reproachful. She stops.
Harsh daylight. THE NARRATOR on the bed, following the pattern hour after hour-gymnastics of the eye. Bloated curves, debased Romanesque with delirium tremens; …
Different light. THE NARRATOR watches the first long straight ray from the east window. The paper changes as she watches.
After meals: enforced lying-down. THE NARRATOR's eyes stay open. She does not sleep. Deceit settles in as habit. JOHN looks in, pleased by her stillness.
When he leaves, she sits up and stares at the wall again.
THE NARRATOR pretends sleep, then rises on innocent excuses. She catches JOHN looking at the wallpaper. Later-JENNIE with her hand on it.
THE NARRATOR brighter, secret energy. She eats. JOHN laughs, relieved.
Fog and rain against the barred windows. A yellow smell hangs in the room-gentle at first, then enduring, awful. THE NARRATOR wakes and finds it hanging over he…
THE NARRATOR descends slowly. The yellow smell seems to wait on the treads. She touches the banister, then her hair.
Moonlight. The front pattern moves because the woman behind shakes it. Sometimes many women; sometimes one, crawling fast. In bright spots she keeps still; in s…
From the nursery windows, THE NARRATOR's POV: a woman creeping in the long shaded lane; creeping in the dark grape arbors; creeping the long road under the tree…
THE NARRATOR locks the door. Alone, she creeps along the smooch-line on the floor, shoulder fitted to the mark. She rises quickly when a board creaks-John must …
Last full night. JOHN stays in town. JENNIE offers to sleep in; THE NARRATOR refuses, sweet and firm.
JENNIE goes. Moonlight. The poor thing behind the pattern crawls and shakes. THE NARRATOR leaps up to help-pulls while the figure shakes, shakes while it pulls.…
Moving day tomorrow. Furniture already going downstairs. The room empties toward bareness: the great bedstead nailed down, canvas mattress left. JENNIE stares a…
JENNIE tries to coax her out. THE NARRATOR resists.
She fastens the rope-secures herself to the room's logic-and begins to creep, shoulder in the long smooch, round and round on the bare floor.
Pounding at the door. JOHN's voice.
THE NARRATOR keeps creeping.
She repeats it gently, slowly, often. At last his footsteps retreat, return. The key turns. JOHN enters-stops short by the door.
THE NARRATOR keeps creeping. She looks at him over her shoulder.
